recital.text: The Stockbridge-Munsee Community is a federally recognized Indian tribe with inherent sovereign powers and is governed by the Stockbridge-Munsee Tribal Council pursuant to the 1937 Constitution, as amended; and
The Stockbridge-Munsee Community has the authority under its Constitution and its inherent rights as a sovereign nation to adopt and amend Ordinances; and
Chapter 57, the Construction Sales Tax Ordinance, was adopted in 1990 by Tribal Council resolution and has not been amended since; and
Chapter 57-A, the Room Tax Ordinance, was adopted in 2010 by Tribal Council Resolution and has not been amended since; and
The proposed revisions to Chapter 57 have been posted for public comment for thirty (30) days and no comments were submitted; and
The authority to enact a sales tax is vested in the Stockbridge-Munsee Community by its Constitution and By-Laws, and particularly by Article VII, Powers; and
The revised Chapter 57 Tax Ordinance establishes a comprehensive tribal tax regime that incorporates both a sales tax and a hotel occupancy tax, as well as the related administrative process.
the Stockbridge-Munsee Tribal Council hereby adopts the following amendments to Chapter 57 and 57-A which include the following changes:

ul.li: Rescind Existing Chapter 57 Construction Sales and Chapter 57-A Room Tax Ordinance and replace with comprehensive Chapter 57 Tax Ordinance; and
Addition and incorporation of an Occupancy Tax, Sales Tax and administrative process into revised Chapter 57.
